SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.io

Interface ObjectStore

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, ReadableObjectStore, WritableObjectStore
All Known Implementing Classes:
FileObjectStore
public interface ObjectStore extends ReadableObjectStore, WritableObjectStore
Represents a generic object storage interface for managing and interacting with objects stored at specified URIs. The interface provides methods to list objects, read and write data, manage folders, and perform other common storage operations.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static final record 
    ObjectStore.ObjectInfo
    A record representing metadata information about an object in an ObjectStore.
    static enum 
    ObjectStore.ObjectType
    Enumeration representing the type of objects.
    static enum 
    ObjectStore.OutputOption
    Specifies options for writing data to a specified output location.

    Deletes the object or folder located at the specified URI path within the object store.

    Methods inherited from interface com.dua3.utility.io.WritableObjectStore

  • Method Details

    • removeFolder

      void removeFolder(URI path) throws IOException
      Removes the specified folder from the object store. Fails if the folder is not empty.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the path of the folder to be removed; must not be an absolute URI and must point within the root of the storage.
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      NotAFolderException - if the path does not point to a folder
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during the removal process.

    • delete

      void delete(URI path) throws IOException
      Deletes the object or folder located at the specified URI path within the object store. If the specified path corresponds to a non-empty folder, an IOException is thrown.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the location of the object or folder to be deleted
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while performing the delete operation

    • deleteRecursively

      default void deleteRecursively(URI path) throws IOException
      Deletes an object at the specified path recursively. If the path refers to a folder, all its contents, including subfolders, are deleted. If the path refers to a file, the file is deleted. If any deletion fails, the operation aborts and the store may be partially modified.
      Parameters:
      path - the URI representing the location of the object or folder to be deleted
      Throws:
      AbsolutePathException - if the path is absolute
      IllegalPathException - if the path points outside the root of the storage
      ObjectNotFoundException - if the path does not point to an existing object
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while deleting the contents
